name: Which types of doors and hardware are best if I want better home security?
headline: Which types of doors and hardware are best if I want better home security?

mainEntity.name: Which types of doors and hardware are best if I want better home security?
mainEntity.text: Which types of doors and hardware are best if I want better home security?

acceptedAnswer.text: Metal and fiberglass exterior doors are generally more secure than basic hollow-core wood or composite doors. A strong door material, combined with solid locks and good installation, can make it harder for someone to force entry. In busy communities like Daly City and San Bruno, many homeowners choose steel or fiberglass for main entries. Security isn’t just about the door panel, though. The door frame, strike plate, hinges, and screws play a huge role. Long screws that reach into the wall framing and reinforced strike plates help prevent the door frame from splitting during forced entry. If you add glass, choose small insulated panels or designs with limited reach to the lock, and use tempered or laminated safety glass for better resistance. Deadbolts should be high quality and installed correctly. Pitfalls include installing a strong door in a weak frame, or using decorative but flimsy hardware. Also, if the door doesn’t close tightly due to poor alignment, it may not latch fully, which weakens security. Don’t forget side and back doors; intruders often target these first. When planning an installation, talk about security with your installer. Make sure they use appropriate hardware and fasteners and pay attention to how the door and frame connect to the surrounding wall. A strong, well-installed door can significantly improve your home’s security.

description: Metal and fiberglass exterior doors are generally more secure than basic hollow-core wood or composite doors. A strong door material, combined with solid locks
